description.task: Build a Java program that generates a simplified OpenAPI 3.0 specification by reading annotated endpoint definitions. Output the spec as structured text.
instructions: In generateSpec(), iterate all declared methods of apiClass.
For each method annotated with @ApiOperation, print path entry: method name as endpoint name, summary, description, responseCode, responseDescription.
Format: each method printed as '  /[methodName]:' followed by indented summary, description, and response.
description.hint: Springdoc/Swagger reads @Operation, @ApiResponse from Spring controllers and generates OpenAPI JSON automatically. Access it at /swagger-ui.html or /v3/api-docs.
description.expectedOutput:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: UserApi Documentation
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /getUsers:
    summary: Get all users
    description: Returns a list of all registered users
    response: 200 - Success
  /createUser:
    summary: Create user
    description: Creates a new user account
    response: 201 - User created
  /deleteUser:
    summary: Delete user
    description: Deletes a user by ID
    response: 204 - No Content

description.task: Implement three API versioning strategies in Java: URL versioning (/api/v1/users), header versioning (Accept: application/vnd.api.v2+json), and query param versioning (?version=3).
instructions: resolveUrlVersion() uses regex to find /v{N}/ in the URL and returns 'vN'.
resolveHeaderVersion() gets the Accept header value, finds 'vnd.api.v(.+?)\\+json' pattern and returns 'v' + the match.
description.hint: URL versioning is most common and explicit. Header versioning is 'cleaner' per REST purists but harder to test. Spring supports all three — URL versioning with path variables is simplest.

description.task: Create a Java program that simulates reading Swagger/OpenAPI-like annotations from a REST controller and generating human-readable API documentation.
description.context: Springdoc-openapi generates interactive API docs from @Operation, @Parameter, @ApiResponse annotations. Good API documentation reduces developer onboarding time significantly.
instructions: In generate(), for each method with @Operation, print the method name, summary, description.
If @ApiResponse exists, print the response code and description.
For each method parameter with @Parameter annotation, print parameter name, description, and required status.
description.hint: Add springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui to pom.xml and your Spring Boot app gets Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html. No extra code needed — it reads existing annotations.
description.expectedOutput:
=== API Documentation: ProductController ===
Endpoint: getProduct
  Summary: Get product by ID
  Description: Retrieves a single product by its unique identifier
  Response: 200 - Product found
  Parameters:
    - id (required): The product ID
Endpoint: searchProducts
  Summary: Search products
  Description: Search products by name with pagination
  Response: 200 - Products matching search criteria
  Parameters:
    - q (optional): Search keyword
    - page (optional): Page number (0-based)
